No Chemicals. No Shortcuts. Natural Food.
Grown with natural practices in living soil with indigenous seeds. Freshly harvested and delivered to your door, weekly.
A Question Worth Asking

Do you remember the mango that actually tasted like a mango?

Your grandparents might have. When food made you feel strong — not just full. That food came from soil that was alive.

Now think about what you ate today. Do you know which soil it came from? How many days in cold storage — waxed, treated, made to look fresh?

We have confused shine with health.

Our vegetables look perfect. A full plate which feels like nourishment. Yet they are delivering pesticides to our children's bodies with every bite.

The average Indian vegetable today travels through cold storage, ripening chambers, and chemical wax before it reaches your hand. The pesticide residue on what you call "fresh vegetables" would fail safety standards in many countries.

This is not fear-mongering. This is what happens when a generation decided that cheap and convenient was the same as good.

The most dangerous thing? It looks completely normal.

What the Data Says

These are not opinions. These are facts — about your food, right now.

67%
of Indian vegetablescarry pesticide residues above safe limits — tested by FSSAI
58%
of Indian farmersare in debt — trapped in a cycle of chemicals they cannot afford to stop
3×
more cancer casesin India's farming states vs. national average — linked to agrochemical exposure

Three Problems. One root.

🌍

The Soil is Dying

What grows in dead soil is not food — it is the shape of food. It looks right. It weighs right. But the nutrition is gone. And what we lose in the soil, we pay for in our bodies.

💧

The Chemicals Don't Stay

They seep into groundwater. They rise as vapour. They travel into the air we breathe and the water we drink. Our bodies are keeping score.

👶

Our Children Inherit This

Children developing allergies and hormonal conditions that didn't exist a generation ago. We call it lifestyle disease. But the lifestyle hasn't changed that much. The food has.

"यथा अन्नं,
तथा मन्नः"
Yatha Anna, thatha Mann — As is the food, so is the mind
Our Philosophy

Food is not just what we eat — it is the balance between all five elements. When Prithvi, Jal, Agni, Vayu, and Akasha are in harmony, that is what nature is truly about.

Our ancestors farmed in conversation with nature — not in combat with it. The result was food that healed. Soil that grew richer every season.

🌍 Prithvi💧 Jal🔥 Agni💨 Vayu✨ Akasha
Our Way

Farming the ancient way, for the modern family.

  • 🌱

    Indigenous Seeds

    Seeds preserved across generations — carrying the memory of thousands of seasons. We grow only what the land already knows.

  • 🐄

    Desi Cow as Pillar

    Only gomutra and compost — nothing else. The desi cow is the foundation of everything we grow. No synthetic fertilizer. Ever.

  • 🌿

    Pest Management, not Killing

    We use repellents to manage pests — not kill them. Every creature has its role in the balance we are trying to restore.

  • 🍃

    Only What the Season Allows

    Nothing forced. Nothing artificial. We grow with the season — because that is when the food is most alive.

Rishi Krishi

When Prithvi, Jal, Agni, Vayu, and Akasha are in harmony — the soil lives. When the soil lives, the food heals. When the food heals, so do we.

Natural farming teaches that health is not a product to be purchased. It flows naturally when all five elements are in balance.

Aarogya Bhoomi is our attempt to restore that balance — one farm at a time.

Living SoilZero ChemicalsSeasonal OnlyFarmer Dignity

A Different Kind of Partnership

Why does the farmer carry this alone?

Think about the person who grew what you ate today. He was in the field before the sun rose. He watched the sky nervously when the monsoon was late. He took loans to buy seeds — more loans when the harvest didn't cover the last ones.

And at the end of all that — he got paid less than what it cost him to grow.

What if this didn't have to be his burden alone?

What if the people who eat the food also shared the responsibility of growing it? Not as charity. Not as sympathy. As equals.

🌿 Only 300 Slots Available

This is the Co-Farmer Model

You share the real cost of growing honest food — directly, with no middleman, no markup. We farm with full natural practices. Every week, fresh harvest reaches your home. Come to the farm. Watch seeds go into the ground. Harvest with your hands. Let your children touch the soil.

When the harvest is good, we celebrate together. When it isn't — we bear the loss together.

We want farming to become a dignified career — chosen with pride, not forced by circumstance. Every family that joins makes the farmer's path a little less lonely. Makes the soil a little more alive.

At the Farm

Sohna Farm, Gurgaon

  Honest Comparison

Market vegetables vs.
Aarogya Bhoomi

What you're actually bringing home.

What you're comparing🛒 Market Vegetables🌿 Aarogya Bhoomi
Purpose of growingSpeed & quantityNutrition & taste
Inputs usedChemical fertilizers & pesticides — many banned in Europe100% natural — gomutra & compost only
How it reaches youCold storage, ripening chambers, chemical wax coatingHarvested and delivered — no storage, no treatment
AvailabilitySame vegetables all year (forced, artificial)Seasonal only — naturally grown
Your relationshipAnonymous. You don't know the soil or the farmer.You know the farmer. You visit the farm. You trust what you eat.
What your child eatsPesticide residues accumulating in small bodiesClean, seasonal, alive — the way a child's body was designed
Who bears farming riskFarmer alone, under crushing debt pressureShared between co-farmers and the farm
What you're really paying forThe appearance of freshnessReal food, honest farming, a future your children can eat from

"Cheap vegetables fill the stomach.
Real vegetables nourish the body."

Market vegetables are not just less nutritious — they actively work against your health, your farmer, and your soil. Aarogya Bhoomi is simply food the way it was always meant to be.

Farm Share Plans

Choose your depth of connection

You are not buying vegetables. You are joining a farm.

Soil Starter
3 Months · Trial
Perfect to taste real food for the first time.
  • Weekly seasonal vegetables & fruits share (5–8 kg)
  • Good for a family of 4
  • Monthly farm visit
  • Kids planting activity
  • Farm updates & crop calendar
Start Here →
Most Popular
Root Sharer
Yearly · Full Partnership
Feels like your own farm without leaving the city.
  • Bigger weekly harvest (8–10 kg/week)
  • Weekly seasonal vegetables & fruits share
  • 2 farm visits per month
  • Work-from-farm day
  • Kids workshops & festival baskets
Join the Farm →
Earth Guardian
Yearly · Extended
For those who want to go deepest into the soil and lead from the front.
  • Everything in Root Sharer
  • Additional herbs, flowers & seasonal gifts
  • Seed saving & learning sessions
  • Open passes for all farm events
  • Name on Co-Farmer Wall
  • Priority in new farm projects
  • Farm stay experience
  • Early access to new farm products
Be a Guardian →

It becomes a relationship.

The poison stops
with your generation.

  From the Soil to Your Table

How your food is grown — naturally.

No shortcuts. No storage. No pretending. Just the honest journey from seed to plate.

🌱

Seed Selection

Indigenous, open-pollinated seeds chosen for nutrition and flavour, not appearance.

🐄

Soil Preparation

Gomutra, compost, and deep care. We feed the soil — not the plant. Living soil does the rest.

🌾

Natural Growth

Grown slowly with the season. Pest management, not killing. Every creature plays its role.

🚚

Fresh to Your Door

Harvested and delivered — no cold storage, no wax, no ripening chambers.

Meet the Visionaries

The Soil Keepers

From living soil to healthy food — naturally.

The Founding Team
Aarogya Bhoomi · Thynaa Healthy Naturally
Three people who left comfortable careers because they believed something simpler and truer was possible. United by the conviction that food is medicine — and that the farm is the first step to healing both the land and the people who eat from it.
Sanchit Aggarwal
Founder · Sustainable Systems & Strategy
With 14+ years building and scaling 0-to-1 startups across technology and social innovation. His work at Thynaa focuses on enabling natural farming, sustainable systems, and community-led food ecosystems.
Tripti Tayal
Co-Founder · Science & Food Integrity
With 10+ years in food regulation and compliance. A background in Biochemistry and Biotechnology, with strong scientific and regulatory expertise in building safe and sustainable food systems.
Arunima Sharma
Co-Founder · Community & Social Transformation
With 15+ years of corporate experience. An ardent meditation practitioner who loves working towards social change. Designs every farm visit, workshop, and harvest festival with deep connection in mind.

Common Questions

Everything you want to know before you join.

You share the real cost of growing your food directly with the farm — no middlemen, no markup. In return, you receive fresh weekly harvests, farm access, and a genuine relationship with the people who grow your food. When harvests are good, you celebrate. When they aren't, you share the responsibility.
Vegetables and fruits are harvested and delivered directly to your home weekly — no cold storage, no artificial ripening, no chemical treatment. What you receive was in the ground just days or hours earlier.
Yes — and we encourage it. Depending on your plan, you get monthly or fortnightly farm visits. Bring your family. Let your children harvest their own food. Do shramdaan. Every visit is an open experience, not a packaged tour.
That is part of what it means to be a co-farmer. Farming is seasonal and uncertain. If a week's harvest is smaller than usual, you receive what the farm can honestly give. We communicate transparently — always.
We follow practices that go beyond what certification typically covers — Rishi Krishi, indigenous seeds, desi cow inputs, no synthetic chemicals. We believe certification should follow trust, not replace it. Visit the farm anytime and see for yourself.
20 families have joined so far. We have 300 slots from our current farms. Once filled, we expand responsibly — not quickly. We would rather do this right than fast.
We currently serve select areas in the NCR region. Fill the join form and our team will confirm delivery availability within 24 hours.
